Japanese: ‥‥事件が起こったとき、 《すでに雪がやんでいた》‥‥
English: ...You claim that the snow had already stopped when the murder occurred.

Japanese: ザンネンですが、あやめさん。 それは、あり得ません。
English: But I'm sorry, Iris. That just isn't possible.
What...!?

Japanese: こいつは、事件当夜の 気象データです。
English: This is the weather data from the night of the murder.

Japanese: 雪がやんだのは、記録によると 午後１０時５０分ごろ‥‥
English: According to this, the snow didn't stop until 10:50 PM.

Japanese: この時間、あなたが おぼろ橋をわたれたハズがない。
English: But you couldn't have crossed Dusky Bridge at that time.

Japanese: ど‥‥‥どうしてですか！
English: W-Why do you say that!?

Japanese: 雪がやむ、５分ほど前‥‥
English: Because 5 minutes before the snow stopped...

Japanese: おぼろ橋は落雷を受けて、 炎上していたからです。
English: ...Dusky Bridge was struck by lightning and had caught on fire.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What did you say!?

Japanese: あ、あの吊り橋が‥‥ 燃えていた‥‥？
English: Th-The bridge... It was on fire...?

Japanese: まさか‥‥知らないわけでは ないでしょうな？
English: You don't mean to say... you didn't know about it?

Japanese: その落雷によって‥‥あの橋は 燃え落ちてしまったのですから。
English: It was because of that lightning strike that the bridge burned down!

Japanese: ええええええッ！　そんな‥‥ ま‥‥まさか！
English: Whaaaaaat!? But it can't... It can't be...!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥まだ、あなたには わかってないようですね。
English: It looks like you still haven't figured it out.

Japanese: どんなに上手にウソを ついたつもりでも‥‥
English: No matter how hard you try to deceive or conceal the truth...

Japanese: 弁護士の目を逃れることは できないんですよ！
English: ...you can't pull the wool over the eyes of a real defense attorney!

Japanese: ‥‥いやああああァァァッ！
English: ...Nooooooooo!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、 すでに橋は燃えていた！
English: The bridge was already on fire when the incident took place.

Japanese: そう！　すでに奥の院は、 世界から切りはなされていた！
English: That's right! The Inner Temple was already totally cut off from the outside world!

Japanese: 橋をわたって、死体を運び出す ことは、不可能だったのです！
English: There's no way she could have crossed the bridge, body or no body!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ぐううううッ！
English: Uh... Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 証人！　‥‥《私のカオも 三度まで》ですぞッ！
English: Witness! Even my patience has its limits.

Japanese: いつわりの証言には、 法廷侮辱罪が適用されます。
English: Any further lying and I will find you in contempt of court!

Japanese: 何か言うことはありますかッ！
English: Do you have anything to say for yourself!?

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: Ugggh...

Japanese: 法廷を侮辱しているのは むしろ、アンタのほうだぜ‥‥
English: The only person here that is truly contemptible is you...

Japanese: なあ‥‥、ジイさんよォ！
English: ...old man!

Japanese: え！　わ、私‥‥ですか！
English: Me!? How dare you!?

Japanese: この証人の証言が、ウソかホントか ‥‥ちっぽけなモンダイさ。
English: Whether this witness lied or not doesn't mean squat right now.

Japanese: ちちち、ちっぽけ‥‥！
English: S-S-Squat!?

Japanese: それより、重要なコトは‥‥ 《真実》だぜ。‥‥そうだろう？
English: The important thing now is to find out the truth. ...Isn't that right?

Japanese: そ‥‥それはそうですが‥‥
English: Y-Yes, of course. But...

Japanese: 雪が降ろうが、雷が落ちようが、 橋が燃えようが‥‥
English: Whether it was snowing, or not snowing, or whether the bridge was burning or not...

Japanese: ゼッタイに動かせない ジジツが２つ、ある。
English: There are 2 facts that can't be disputed.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスの死体が、葉桜院の 境内で発見されていること。
English: First, the body of Elise Deauxnim was discovered in the Hazakura Temple Courtyard.

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。 あの住職は、目撃しているのさ。
English: And second, the head nun, Sister Bikini...

Japanese: この証人が、死体に 細工しているところをなァ！
English: ...witnessed Iris desecrating Elise Deauxnim's body!
(H-He makes a good point... on both accounts.)

Japanese: そ‥‥、そうですわ！　私‥‥ ウソなんて、ついていません！
English: Th-That's right! I'm not lying!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What are you claiming this time!?

Japanese: あの晩の私は‥‥ フツウではありませんでした。
English: I wasn't myself at all that night.

Japanese: ‥‥記憶が少し、 コンランしているんです！
English: ...So my memory is still somewhat hazy!

Japanese: あなたは《証人》として そこに立っているのです！
English: You have stood at that witness stand and testified this entire time!

Japanese: 今さら、“コンラン”なんて‥‥
English: Are you telling us now that your memory of that night is hazy...?

Japanese: ウソをつくのも人間なら‥‥ カンちがいするのも、人間だ。
English: It is only human to err.

Japanese: ‥‥それなら、まるほどう。 アンタには、説明できるのかい？
English: If you're so perfect, Trite, maybe you can explain this for the court?

Japanese: な、何をですか‥‥！
English: Wh-What is it...?

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、すでに おぼろ橋は燃えていた。
English: When the murder happened, the bridge had already burnt down.

Japanese: しかし‥‥死体は橋をわたり、 葉桜院の境内で発見された。
English: But somehow the body traveled across the bridge and was found in the temple courtyard.

Japanese: その《カラクリ》を‥‥ カンペキに立証できるのかい！
English: Perhaps you have some kind of perfect explanation for this little magic trick?

Japanese: ぐっ‥‥！ そ、それは‥‥‥
English: Ugh...! Well not exactly, no...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥証言はどうあれ 死体は、炎上した橋をわたった）
English: (I know there must be some other way she got across that burnt-out bridge.)

Japanese: （その方法が立証できなければ ‥‥真相は、見えない！）
English: (But unless I can somehow demonstrate it... We'll never know the truth!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人には 説明できないようですね‥‥
English: It looks like the defense is not prepared to offer a suitable explanation...

Japanese: そういうことだ。 ‥‥つまり‥‥
English: You see what I mean? In other words...

Japanese: アンタは、この証人をウソつき 呼ばわりする資格はないのさ！
English: You're in no position to suggest that this lady's testimony isn't the truth!

Japanese: ぐおおおおォォッ！
English: Aaaaaack!

Japanese: それでは‥‥証人。 もう一度だけ、証言してください。
English: Alright then. Witness, let's hear your testimony once more.

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥なんでしょうか？
English: About what, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたは、死体を移動させた罪を 告白なさいました。
English: You've admitted that you moved the victim's body.

Japanese: ただ、先ほどの証言がムジュン していたのは、ジジツです。
English: Nevertheless, your prior testimony contained a rather large inconsistency.

Japanese: ‥‥最後に、その説明を おねがいしましょうか。
English: ...Please add an explanation for that to your testimony.

Japanese: ええ。わかりましたわ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （‥‥これが‥‥ 最後の証言、か‥‥！）
English: (...Will this be her final testimony...!?)

Japanese: ～死体を運んだこと～
English: -- Moving the Body --

Japanese: 橋をわたる以外に、死体を運ぶ 方法は、あり得ません。
English: Other than walking over the bridge, there's no way to move the body.

Japanese: だから‥‥きっと、私‥‥ カンちがいしていたのですね。
English: So I... must have just... gotten confused, I guess.

Japanese: 雪が降っていたか、どうか‥‥？ そんなの、ささいなコトです。
English: Was the snow still falling or had it stopped...? Does it really matter that much?

Japanese: それとも‥‥燃え上がった橋を わたる方法が、あるとでも‥‥？
English: Or are you saying that there is a way to cross a burning bridge?

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。やはり カンちがい、だったのですかな。
English: Hmm... So it was just a misunderstanding, I see.

Japanese: ここに、落雷によって燃えた おぼろ橋の写真があるぜ。
English: This is a photo of Dusky Bridge after it burned down from the lightning blast.

Japanese: 事件の翌朝、撮影されたものだ。
English: It was taken on the morning after the incident.

Japanese: これは‥‥ミゴトに 燃えつきていますね‥‥。
English: It certainly was burned to a crisp...

Japanese: 支柱がわりのワイヤーも切れて‥‥ よく、橋が落ちなかったものです。
English: And one of the suspension wires is gone... It's amazing the whole bridge didn't fall.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥これでは、死体を 運ぶのは不可能でしょうな。
English: Clearly it would be impossible to carry a corpse across a bridge in this condition.

Japanese: 証拠品《おぼろ橋の写真》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Dusky Bridge Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （この証言をくつがえすことが できなければ‥‥）
English: (Unless I do something to discredit this testimony...)

Japanese: （証言は“正しい”ことに なってしまう‥‥）
English: (...it's going to be deemed as the truth...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが、《殺人者》に されてしまうんだ‥‥！）
English: (...and Maya will be accused of murder!)

Japanese: いいか、まるほどう‥‥ もう一度、言っておくぜ。
English: Trite. I'm only going to say it one more time.

Japanese: カンちがいするのも人間なら‥‥ ウソをつくのも、人間だ。
English: It is only human to err.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And...

Japanese: そのウソを見破るのも‥‥ やっぱり、人間なのさ。
English: ...only humans can spot the errors of our ways.

Japanese: （‥‥わかったような、 わからないような‥‥）
English: (The more sense he makes, the less sense he makes...)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ 最後の尋問をおねがいします！
English: Alright, Mr. Wright! Please begin your final cross-examination.

Japanese: ～死体を運んだこと～
English: -- Moving the Body --

Japanese: 橋をわたる以外に、死体を運ぶ 方法は、あり得ません。
English: Other than walking over the bridge, there's no way to move the body.

Japanese: だから‥‥きっと、私‥‥ カンちがいしていたのですね。
English: So I... must have just... gotten confused, I guess.

Japanese: 雪が降っていたか、どうか‥‥？ そんなの、ささいなコトです。
English: Was the snow still falling or had it stopped...? Does it really matter that much?

Japanese: それとも‥‥燃え上がった橋を わたる方法が、あるとでも‥‥？
English: Or are you saying that there is a way to cross a burning bridge?

Japanese: （‥‥ここまで来たら、なんと しても立証しなければならない）
English: (I've come all this way, so I owe it to everyone to figure this out...)

Japanese: （炎上した橋の上を、どうやって 死体は運ばれたか‥‥？）
English: (But how do you transport a body across a burning bridge...?)

Japanese: （ハッキリしたコタエが、まだ わからなくても、かまわない）
English: (Even if I don't know the exact answer yet, it's OK.)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥すべては 《証拠品》から始まる‥‥）
English: (I'll start by examining all of my evidence, and see where that takes me...)

Japanese: （立ち止まったら、負けだ。 ‥‥走りながら、考えよう！）
English: (I've gotta be like a shark... If I don't keep moving, I'm as good as dead!)

Japanese: しかし！　事件が起こったとき、 雪がやんでいたのなら‥‥
English: Yes, but if the snow had already stopped when the incident took place...

Japanese: 橋をわたることは、できなかった。 ‥‥当然ですよね。
English: I couldn't have crossed the bridge. ...Naturally.

Japanese: 私‥‥、検事さまのおっしゃる とおりだと思います。
English: But... I think it's just like Mr. Godot said...

Japanese: 死体はジッサイ、 橋をわたっているのですから‥‥
English: The body did make it across somehow...

Japanese: しかし‥‥さっきの証言で、 あなたは断言したはずです！
English: But you seemed to be quite sure of yourself when you gave your testimony!

Japanese: 『たしかに、雪はやんでいた』 ‥‥と‥‥
English: You said you were sure the snow had already stopped.

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありませんでした。
English: I'm terribly sorry... I was wrong.

Japanese: （‥‥そのヒトコトで 済ませるつもりらしいな）
English: (Does she really think an apology is going to get her off the hook?)

Japanese: 冗談じゃありません！ それが、事件のポイントなのです！
English: This is no joke! You're giving sworn testimony in a court of law!

Japanese: それなら‥‥成歩堂さま。 あなたは、おぼえていますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright... Let me ask you a question.

Japanese: １週間前のお天気を ‥‥ハッキリと？
English: Do you remember what the weather was like one week ago today?

Japanese: え‥‥！　それは、モチロン‥‥
English: Huh...!? W-Well, I... Naturally, it was, umm...

Japanese: ね。‥‥そんなものですわ。 ヒトの記憶、などというものは‥‥
English: You see? It's not that easy to remember, is it?

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも！　それが特別な日 なら、キチンとおぼえています！
English: ...B-But if it had been some kind of a special day, I would have remembered!

Japanese: たとえば！　ぼくの小学校の 入学式は、ドシャ降りでした！
English: For example, it was pouring rain on the day of my elementary school graduation!

Japanese: アンタのドシャ降り人生のコト なんざ、ダレも聞いてねえ。
English: No one wants to hear about your childhood traumas, Trite.
What...!?

Japanese: 《事件があったとき、すでに 雪がやんでいた》
English: "By the time the murder took place, the snow had already stopped."

Japanese: その証言にこだわるなら‥‥ 提示することだ。
English: If you insist on obsessing over that one statement, let's see the proof.

Japanese: 炎上した橋を死体がわたった‥‥ そのジジツを示す《証拠品》を！
English: Show some evidence that the body somehow crossed the bridge while it was on fire!

Japanese: ‥‥ゼッタイにないとは 言い切れません！
English: ...Well, I don't see why there couldn't be a way across!

Japanese: 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: これは、子どものケンカじゃ ありませんのよ。
English: Don't you think that sounds a bit silly?

Japanese: ‥‥燃え上がっている橋を、 死者がわたった‥‥
English: A dead body crossing a burning bridge...

Japanese: そんな光景を目撃したヒトでも いるというのかしら‥‥？
English: Did anyone actually witness such a thing...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
そいつはきっと、 《目撃者》とは呼べねえだろうぜ。
English: If there was, I don't think you'd call someone like that a witness!

Japanese: “夢”は、目で見るものじゃ ねえからなァ‥‥
English: After all, a dream isn't the same as actually seeing something...

Japanese: 《燃え上がる橋の上を 死者が飛び越えた》‥‥
English: A dead body flying over a burning bridge...

Japanese: その可能性は‥‥ ゼロではありません。
English: I wouldn't exactly rule out the possibility.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 可能性は、ゼロじゃねえ‥‥か。
English: Ha...! You're saying it's possible? Don't make me laugh.

Japanese: しかし。‥‥そのコンキョはゼロ、 だろうぜ。
English: The only thing that's possible about your claim is that it's been pulled out of thin air!

Japanese: それは、どうでしょうか。
English: I don't know about that.

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥ 目撃者がいましたからね。
English: In any case... We have a witness who did see it happen.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだとほォォォッ！
English: ...Pwweposterous!

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥その 《目撃者》とは、ダレですか！
English: Wh-Who is it!? Who is this witness!?

Japanese: （ここから先は‥‥もう、 イキオイで攻めるしかない！）
English: (I can't chicken out here... I've gotta keep on the attack and go, go, go!)

Japanese: ‥‥天流斎　マシス。 天才絵本作家のタマゴですッ！
English: Ms. Elise Deauxnim's brilliant and highly-gifted apprentice, Laurice Deauxnim!

Japanese: てんさい‥‥？
English: Brilliant...?

Japanese: えほんさっか‥‥？
English: Highly-gifted...?

Japanese: おたまご‥‥？
English: Apprentice...?

Japanese: ‥‥彼の証言を 思い出してください。
English: ...Remember what he said in his testimony.

Japanese: 彼は、あの晩‥‥現場付近の 山小屋にいました。
English: That night he was at the mountain shack, Heavenly Hall.

Japanese: そして‥‥その“瞬間”を 目撃したのです！
English: And that's when he witnessed the event.

Japanese: みなさんも、このスケッチは ご存じでしょう！
English: I think you've all seen this sketch before!

Japanese: ‥‥彼が目撃した光景を、 そのまま描いたものです！
English: It's an exact drawing of what he witnessed that night!

Japanese: ‥‥あなた‥‥ホンキで 主張するつもりですか？
English: ...Are you serious? Today's not April Fools' Day, is it?

Japanese: 被害者が‥‥ 《空を飛んだ》などとッ！
English: Mr. Wright. Are you seriously claiming that the victim flew through the air!?

Japanese: この、メルヘンティックな ラクガキをコンキョにッ！
English: And you're using this pathetic scribble to support your argument!?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥裁判長、クチビルが ぶるぶるフルえているぞ‥‥）
English: (Uh-oh... The judge looks like he's about to blow a gasket.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥
ここまで来て、引っこめるのは ナシ、だぜ‥‥
English: Well, Trite. There's nowhere for you to hide now...

Japanese: ‥‥このラクガキを使って 立証してもらおうじゃねえか。
English: Other than looking like it was drawn by a 6 year old, does this sketch prove anything?

Japanese: わ‥‥わかっています！
English: Y-Yes... I'm pretty sure it does, and I'm going to prove it!

Japanese: アンタ‥‥発想を逆転させるのが トクイなんだってなァ‥‥
English: Listen... I know your tricks. You're trying to turn this whole thing upside-down.

Japanese: いっそ‥‥逆転させてみるかい？ このラクガキも‥‥
English: If you're so eager to turn this case upside-down, why not start with this sketch?

Japanese: （《逆転》‥‥なぜ、 ゴドー検事がそれを‥‥‥）
English: (Upside-down...? Why did Godot say that...?)

Japanese: やっぱり、ぼくには これしかありません！
English: It all comes back to this!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。また、その いまわしい証拠品ですか‥‥
English: I can't believe we're looking at this again...

Japanese: たしかに、さっきはまだ、 よくわかっていませんでした。
English: I admit that I didn't really understand it before...

Japanese: しかし‥‥。
English: But I get it now!

Japanese: スケッチのどこかに 《真実》がひそんでいるのです！
English: That's why I believe this sketch shows the truth!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人の 結論をうかがいましょう！
English: ...Alright then, let's hear the defense's theory.

Japanese: このスケッチに描かれた光景は ‥‥どうなのですかッ！
English: What exactly is this sketch trying to show?

Japanese: （まちがいは、ユルされない！）
English: (I don't think old whisker-face is going to forgive any more mistakes!)

Japanese: （よく見ろ！　そして、 考えるんだ‥‥！）
English: (Alright, Phoenix. Look carefully and think it over!)

Japanese: ‥‥この、天流斎　マシスの スケッチは‥‥！
English: This sketch drawn by Laurice Deauxnim is...

Japanese: こんなラクガキ！ なんの証拠にもなりはしないッ！
English: This sketch proves exactly one thing! ...Absolutely nothing!

Japanese: ‥‥長年、このシゴトを やっていますが‥‥
English: I've been a judge for a long time now...

Japanese: 自分の提示した証拠を、ここまで 明快に否定する弁護士は初めて‥‥
English: But I've never seen a defense lawyer so brazenly admit the flimsiness of their evidence!

Japanese: ある種の爽快感を 禁じ得ません。
English: I must say it was quite refreshing in a strange way.

Japanese: 時代は変わる。‥‥長生きは するもんだぜ、ジイさん。
English: Times change, Your Honor. ...Like they say, you are quite the old man, Old Man.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくは、寿命が少しだけ ちぢんだような気がするけど）
English: (...I have the feeling my own life span just got shorter.)

Japanese: もう少し、短くしてやるぜ！ ‥‥アンタの寿命！
English: Allow me to speed you along the aging process, Trite!

Japanese: それでは！　証人には、 証言に戻っていただきましょう！
English: Alright then! The witness will now return to her testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: もちろん、被害者は 空を飛んだのです！
English: Of course the victim was flying through the air!

Japanese: スケッチのとおりに！ ピョーン、って！
English: You can see it right there in the sketch! ...Whooooosh!

Japanese: あなた‥‥きのうの証人に 似てきましたな。
English: You know... You're starting to remind me of yesterday's witness.

Japanese: ぐふおおおおおッ！
English: Whhhhhhhaaaaa!?

Japanese: （‥‥それだけは 言われたくなかった‥‥）
English: (That was the last thing I wanted to hear...)

Japanese: 被害者が空を飛んだという 証拠など、あるのですかッ！
English: Do you have any evidence that the victim flew through the air!?

Japanese: ちなみに‥‥
English: Just so you know...

Japanese: 今のところ、奥の院から 大砲は発見されていねえ。
English: We haven't discovered a giant human catapult at the Inner Temple yet.

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろうな‥‥）
English: (Uggh... I wish they had.)

Japanese: アンタの捨て身の発言は、 コーヒー１杯ぶん、楽しめたぜ。
English: Even if you're a lousy lawyer, at least you're one cup's worth of entertainment.

Japanese: ペナルティはカンベンして おいてやろうか。
English: I think we should skip the penalty this time.

Japanese: ぐううぅぅ‥‥。
English: Nnnnrgh...

Japanese: （‥‥何か‥‥ 何か、方法があるはずだ‥‥）
English: (There must be some way. I just know it...)

Japanese: （アイツが目撃した光景の 《真実》を暴く方法が‥‥）
English: (There must be a way to use Larry's sketch to show the truth of what happened...)

Japanese: この絵は、アキラカに ムジュンしていますッ！
English: Something is obviously funny about this sketch!

Japanese: そんなコトは わかっていますッ！
English: I'm no art critic, but even I can see that!

Japanese: いやいやいや！　そういう イミではありません！
English: No, no! That's not what I mean, Your Honor.

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスは、 何度も証言しています。
English: Laurice Deauxnim stated it over and over...

Japanese: ‥‥このスケッチは、 《見たまま》を描いた、と。
English: That this sketch was exactly as he saw it.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: ...However!

Japanese: その証言を信じると、 この絵の中には‥‥
English: If we're to believe his testimony...

Japanese: 現実とムジュンしている ポイントがあるのです。
English: ...then the sketch contradicts reality as we know it.

Japanese: ムジュン‥‥‥
English: It contradicts reality...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ‥‥おもしれえ。
English: Ha...! This is getting interesting.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、そのコリない 人差し指の登場らしいな。
English: Looks like you're back to that finger-pointing thing again.

Japanese: いったい、このスケッチ‥‥ 現実とムジュンする点は、どこか？
English: OK, Trite. So what exactly contradicts reality as we know it?

Japanese: それはモチロン、この 飛んでいる人影です！
English: This flying figure, naturally!

Japanese: なんせ、ニンゲンが空を飛ぶ なんて、あり得ませんからね！
English: After all, people haven't learned to fly yet... Or did I miss something?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さま。 今のヒトコト‥‥
English: ...Mr. Wright. That answer just now...

Japanese: 致命的ですわ。
English: It was lethal.

Japanese: 弁護人！　ここまで引っぱって、 そのコタエはあんまりです！
English: Mr. Wright! After dragging us all the way to this point, that answer was embarrassing!

Japanese: ‥‥私、情けなくて ナミダが出てきました。
English: It was so embarrassing, I'm actually blushing.

Japanese: （年寄りにナミダは似合わない。 どうするべきか‥‥）
English: (Blushing only suits women, not the elderly. Anyway, what now...?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、最初から 考えなおしてみます。
English: I've decided to rethink the whole thing.

Japanese: それがいいでしょうな。
English: That sounds like a wise idea.

Japanese: イナカに帰る‥‥ それもまた、１つの決断だぜ。
English: Rethinking why you became a lawyer? Perhaps you'd like to rethink merely being born!

Japanese: いやいや！　そこまで さかのぼるつもりはないです‥‥
English: No, no! I'm not going that far back...

Japanese: 裁判長！　もう一度だけ、 チャンスをください！
English: Your Honor! Please give me one more chance!

Japanese: （“発想を逆転させる”‥‥ それでピンチを切り抜けてきた）
English: (I'm in a tight spot now... What I really need to do is turn this case upside-down...)

Japanese: （逆転させるんだ！　発想も‥‥ スケッチも！）
English: (So it's time for me to flip things around and get them straight again in my head...)

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ、大丈夫でしょうな？
English: ...Do you know what you're saying this time?

Japanese: おそらく、このへんだと 思われます！
English: I believe it's somewhere... around here... Maybe.

Japanese: “おそらく”‥‥？
English: "Somewhere"...?

Japanese: “このへん”‥‥？
English: "Around here"...?

Japanese: “思われます”‥‥？
English: "Maybe"...?

Japanese: まあ、この絵の作者が ムジュンだらけのオトコですから。
English: Well, the artist responsible for this sketch is a bit of a walking contradiction.

Japanese: どこでもムジュン、と 言ってもいいかな、と‥‥
English: So it's difficult to point out where the contradictions end and the truth begins...

Japanese: ヒトのせいにしないようにッ！
English: Enough with the psycho-babble!

Japanese: それは‥‥この、 橋から伸びたワイヤーです！
English: It's this wire connected to the bridge!

Japanese: ‥‥ワイヤー、ですか‥‥
English: ...The wire?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ソイツが、“現実”と ムジュンしてる、ってワケかい‥‥
English: Is that the thing that contradicts reality?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: It is indeed.

Japanese: それなら‥‥その“現実”って ヤツを、示してもらおうか。
English: Then show us the reality it supposedly conflicts with.

Japanese: ‥‥このスケッチのワイヤーと ムジュンする《証拠》を‥‥！
English: Show us something that will point out how the sketch contradicts reality!

Japanese: ‥‥これが、その証拠です！
English: ...Here's your evidence!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Trite...

Japanese: アンタの“現実”ってヤツは、 このスケッチていど、ってコトか。
English: It looks like the only piece of reality you have is that poorly drawn sketch.
Huh...?

Japanese: アンタ‥‥、どこかへ飛んで 行っちゃっていいぜ！
English: And the only thing flying away here is your chance of winning this case!

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、ぼくには まだ、見えてないみたいだ）
English: (I guess I just don't get it yet.)

Japanese: （このスケッチに描かれた 《真実》が‥‥）
English: (I still don't understand what the truth behind this sketch is.)

Japanese: さあ、いかがですか？　弁護人。 ‥‥考えなおすなら、今ですぞ。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? If you want to think it over, now is your chance.

Japanese: （まだ、ハッキリしないけど‥‥）
English: (I'm still not too sure...)

Japanese: （ここまでのスイリに、 まちがいはないはずだ！）
English: (But I'm certain I'm in the right ballpark!)

Japanese: （このワイヤーこそが‥‥ 《真実》を暴く、カギだ！）
English: (Somehow this wire is the key to explaining things!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
それは‥‥ おぼろ橋の写真、ですか。
English: That's a photo of Dusky Bridge, correct?

Japanese: 《スケッチ》と《写真》‥‥ よく見くらべてみてください。
English: Yes. Now, compare the sketch and the photo for a minute.

Japanese: スケッチでは、ワイヤーは 《手すりの上》から伸びています。
English: In the sketch, the wires appear to be above the guard wires.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 現実のおぼろ橋では‥‥‥
English: But on the actual Dusky Bridge...

Japanese: あああああああッ！
English: Jumping Jehosaphat!

Japanese: ワイヤーは‥‥‥《手すりの下》 から伸びているのです！
English: The wires are... below the guard wires!

Japanese: ‥‥なにひィィィッ！
English: ...Bwhaaaat!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: たしかに、スケッチは“現実”と 食いちがっているようです！
English: This sketch is somewhat different than what's depicted in the photo!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: これは単なる、 “見まちがい”‥‥なのでは？
English: Isn't it likely that the artist just saw it wrong?

Japanese: “描きまちがい”かも しれませんわ！
English: Or perhaps he just drew it wrong!

Japanese: いっそ、アンタが “場ちがい”だぜ、まるほどう！
English: Either way, it sounds like you're... just wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥このオトコのことですから、 “見まちがい”はあり得ます。
English: ...With someone like Laurice, I admit a mistake is a definite possibility.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 そこで問題になるのは‥‥
English: But then that begs the question...

Japanese: 彼が《なぜ、見まちがえたか》？ ‥‥その“理由”です。
English: "Why did he make a mistake?" What was the reason?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに、それが わかる、って言うのかい‥‥
English: ...Are you saying you know the answer to that?

Japanese: みなさん！ 思い出していただけますか。
English: Listen! Think back, alright?

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスが、あの瞬間を 目撃したときの《状況》を‥‥
English: Remember what Laurice was doing when he witnessed this event.

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩、彼は極楽庵で、来る はずのないコイビトを待っていた。
English: ...He was at Heavenly Hall waiting for a lover that was never going to come.

Japanese: やがて、待ちくたびれた彼は うたた寝を始めます。
English: He waited and waited, and finally he laid down.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき！　運命の落雷で 橋は燃え上がり‥‥
English: ...But then! Lightning shoots from the sky and sets the bridge aflame!

Japanese: 彼は、その瞬間を目撃した。 ‥‥こんな状態でッ！
English: Now ponder what sort of position Larry must have been in at the time...

Japanese: あおむけに見た一瞬の光景が、 彼の目に焼きついてしまった‥‥
English: He was lying on his back, which is why he remembered the scene the way he did.
He was lying on his back...? I can't see how it relates...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 それが《コタエ》だったのです。
English: But it does, Your Honor.

Japanese: なぜ、スケッチのワイヤーが “上下、逆に”伸びていたか‥‥？
English: That is the reason why the wires in the sketch go up instead of down...

Japanese: あああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか‥‥‥ッ！
English: Ugh... No way...!

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスは、あおむけに 寝た状態で、事件を目撃した。
English: Laurice Deauxnim witnessed the event while he was lying on his back, face up.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！ 彼の目撃した光景は‥‥
English: In other words, the scene that he saw...

Japanese: 上下が《逆転》していたのです！
English: ...was actually upside-down!

Japanese: そ、それでは！ ‥‥このスケッチは‥‥
English: So then...! This sketch should actually...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥お気づきになった ようですね。
English: I think you finally get it, Your Honor.

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスの描いた 《スケッチ》の、正しい見かたが！
English: The correct way to view Laurice Deauxnim's sketch is like this!

Japanese: ‥‥これこそが、この絵の 《正しい姿》だったのです！
English: This is how it should actually look!

Japanese: 被害者の死体は、橋の“上空”を 飛んだのではなかった！
English: The victim's body wasn't flying above the bridge!

Japanese: 橋の“下”を、くぐったのです！
English: It was actually swinging below!

Japanese: ‥‥そう！　まるで、 《振り子》のように！
English: ...That's right! Just like a pendulum!

Japanese: ぐぶるほほほおおおおおォォォッ！
English: Bwwweeeediculous!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order--

Japanese: よりによって‥‥ なんで《振り子》なんだッ！
English: Of all the things to say! A pendulum!?

Japanese: ‥‥橋はすでに燃え上がって、 とてもわたれる状況ではなかった。
English: ...The bridge was burning to a crisp. There was no way to get across it.

Japanese: しかし‥‥被害者の死体は、 奥の院で発見されてはならない‥‥
English: But if the body had been found at the Inner Temple, it would have caused problems.

Japanese: そこで犯人は、イチかバチかの 《賭け》をしたのです。
English: This is where the criminal decided to take a gamble.

Japanese: 燃える橋を使って、死体を対岸に わたすためには‥‥
English: They used the burning bridge to get the body across to the other side.

Japanese: 《振り子》を使うしかなかった！
English: And a pendulum was the only way to get it done!

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、地に足をつけて 考えるとするぜ‥‥。
English: ...Let's think about this for a minute, shall we?

Japanese: おぼろ橋の全長は、 約２０メートルもある。
English: Dusky Bridge is about 20 yards long.

Japanese: つまり、《奥の院》から対岸まで、 それだけ離れている、ってワケだ。
English: Which means it's about that far from the Inner Temple to the opposite cliff.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなりますね。
English: ...Yes, that sounds right.

Japanese: そのキョリを《振り子》で クリアするためには‥‥
English: In order to cover that distance with a pendulum...

Japanese: 少なくとも、１０メートルの 長いロープが必要になる。
English: You'd need a rope at least 10 yards long.

Japanese: そんな長いロープなんぞ、 カンタンに用意できるワケがねえ！
English: To get a rope that long, you have to plan ahead!

Japanese: （あの晩、橋に落雷があったのは、 あくまでもグーゼンだった）
English: (The lightning strike that night can only have been an accident.)

Japanese: （だから、犯人が前もってロープを 用意しておいたハズはない）
English: (So it doesn't make sense that the criminal would have prepared the rope beforehand.)

Japanese: （‥‥つまり、犯人は‥‥）
English: (So then, the criminal...)

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ 用意するまでもなかった‥‥
English: They didn't have to get the rope ready...

Japanese: 犯人の目の前に、 《ロープ》はあったのです！
English: The rope was already right in front of the criminal.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: だから、それを使った。 ‥‥それだけのことです。
English: I'm saying that it was just a matter of using what was already there.

Japanese: では、弁護人に 提示していただきましょう！
English: In that case, Mr. Wright, please give us an explanation to support your theory.

Japanese: 犯人が、死体の《振り子》に 使用した、《ロープ》のありかを！
English: What makes you think the criminal had the rope on hand to create a pendulum!?

Japanese: いかがですか、ゴドー検事。
English: What do you think, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ‥‥この場にロープがなくて、 ラッキーだったな、まるほどう‥‥
English: I think that Mr. Trite is lucky there's no rope around here...
Huh...?

Japanese: もし、そんなものがあったら‥‥
English: Even if I had just a sliver of rope...

Japanese: アンタのクビを、きゅきゅっと シメていたところだぜ。
English: ...I'd be wrapping it nice and tight around his neck.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥当てずっぽうでは 勝てそうもないな‥‥）
English: (I should have figured I'm not going to win this one with a lucky guess...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたの主張には ただでさえムリがあります。
English: Mr. Wright, your assertion seems to be baseless...

Japanese: 死体を《振り子》で 運んだ、などと‥‥
English: There's no evidence that the body was swung like a pendulum after all...

Japanese: しかし！　燃え上がった橋を わたる方法は、それしかなかった！
English: Wait! But that's the only possible way the body could have gotten across!

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥？
English: And the meaning of this is...?

Japanese: 犯人が使った《ロープ》なら‥‥
English: If you want to know where the rope came from...

Japanese: 目の前にブラ下がって いるじゃないですか！
English: ...it's hanging right there in front of your glorious beard!

Japanese: ああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: これは‥‥ おぼろ橋のワイヤー‥‥ですか！
English: This... This is one of the wires from the bridge!

Japanese: 落雷によって、橋は炎上した！ そして、その際‥‥
English: When the lightning struck the bridge and set it on fire...

Japanese: ワイヤーのうちの１本が、 留め具からハズれかかっていた！
English: ...one of the suspension wires came loose from its anchor.

Japanese: ‥‥犯人に、迷っている 時間は、ありませんでした。
English: The criminal didn't have any time to waste.

Japanese: そのワイヤーに、死体を 結びつけて‥‥
English: So they tied the wire around Elise Deauxnim's body...

Japanese: これ以外に、死体を運ぶ 方法はありません！
English: ...because there was simply no other way to move the body!

Japanese: うごふごふごふごふごふごふごふ ごふごふごふごふごふごふごふご
English: *glug, glug, glug, glug, glug* *glug, glug, glug, glug, glug* *glug, glug, glug, glug, glug*

Japanese: いかがですか、ゴドー検事！
English: Mr. Godot!?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あまりのコトに、検察側は コトバもないようです。
English: It seems that Mr. Godot is more focused on his coffee than answering my question.

Japanese: たしかに、現場に《ロープ》は ありました。
English: It seems that the odds of a rope being readily available were very high.

Japanese: 《不可能ではない》 ‥‥そう考えていいでしょう！
English: So I suppose that it's not an impossibility after all!

Japanese: 《可能》か《不可能》か‥‥？ そんなコト、問題じゃねえぜ。
English: "Possible" or "impossible"...? That's not the question we need to ask!

Japanese: ‥‥ダイジなのは、１つだけ。 ジッサイに、やったかどうか‥‥？
English: There's only one question. "Did that really happen?"

Japanese: それならば、まるほどう！ ‥‥見せてもらおうじゃねえか。
English: Trite! I wonder if you can prove what happened to us.

Japanese: ジッサイに、死体が《振り子》で 飛ばされたという証拠を！
English: Do you have any actual evidence that the body was swung over like a pendulum!?

Japanese: ‥‥これが、その証拠です！
English: ...Here's the proof!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタが《振り子》で 飛ばされちゃいな。
English: ...Why don't you go swing yourself around like a pendulum, Trite.

Japanese: では、もう一度 考えなおしていただきましょう。
English: I think you should think that over again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、妙に テンポがいいな‥‥）
English: (...It's kinda nice when they don't spend too much time thinking of a witty retort.)

Japanese: ‥‥あなたがまちがうのに、 慣れてきましたからね。
English: I suppose we've gotten used to your mistakes.

Japanese: （‥‥死体が飛ばされた 状況を、よく考えるんだ‥‥）
English: (I need to think about how this body was moved...)

Japanese: （《振り子》は、とっさに 思いついて、実行された）
English: (The criminal decided to use the pendulum method as a last resort...)

Japanese: （死体が着地したとき、何か 手がかりが残った可能性は高い）
English: (So there's a good chance there's still a clue from when or where the body landed.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
そのコリないヒトミに カンパイ‥‥だぜ。
English: Here's a toast to your ever so classic clueless-look.

Japanese: 《証拠》を提示する前に、 少し、整理して考えてみましょう。
English: Before I present my evidence, let me review what we know so far.

Japanese: この写真によると、 切れたワイヤーは、１本。
English: According to this photo, one of the wires snapped.

Japanese: 上面図で見ると、コイツです。 《奥の院》の前につながっていた。
English: Looking at the map, we can see it's the one that was in front of the Inner Temple.

Japanese: では‥‥犯人は、 その場所から死体を‥‥！
English: So then, that was the spot where the criminal...!?

Japanese: もちろん、そうでしょう。
English: Yes, precisely.

Japanese: ‥‥そのときの死体の動きを、 上面図で考えてみましょう。
English: Now let us consider the body's movement by looking at the overhead map again.

Japanese: ‥‥このポイントから 投げ出された死体は‥‥
English: If the body was pushed from this point here...

Japanese: ‥‥対岸の、このあたりに 落下することになります。
English: ...it would drop on the opposite bank at approximately this point.

Japanese: ら‥‥“落下”‥‥ですか？
English: D-Did you say drop?

Japanese: おそらく、対岸で死体を受けとめる のに、シッパイしたのでしょう。
English: Well, they must have failed to catch the body on the other bank.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥そんなことが わかるのですか！
English: What...!? What makes you think something like that happened!?

Japanese: 死体が対岸に“落下”したことを 証明する証拠があるからです。
English: Because I have evidence that suggests her body dropped some distance.

Japanese: そ‥‥ そんな証拠が‥‥！
English: Wh-What kind of evidence!?

Japanese: ‥‥この、《解剖記録》を 見ていただきましょう。
English: ...Take a look at this autopsy report.

Japanese: こう書かれています。 『死後、３ｍの高さを落下』
English: It says here that her body fell about 10 feet after her death.

Japanese: さ‥‥３メートル‥‥
English: Ten feet, huh...

Japanese: おそらく、対岸との高さの差が あったためでしょう。
English: That's most likely the height difference between the two sides.

Japanese: 死体はワイヤーからはずれて、 ３メートルの高さから落ちた。
English: The body over swung due to forward momentum but then came loose and fell about 10 feet.

Japanese: そして、そのときのショックで‥‥
English: And then, as a result of the landing impact...

Japanese: ‥‥この水晶が ハズれてしまったのです！
English: ...this crystal sphere was knocked loose!
That's...

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 この血痕の残った水晶は‥‥
English: Yes, this blood-stained amethyst crystal...

Japanese: 被害者・天流斎　エリスさんの ツエに、はまっていたものです！
English: It's the one that came off of Ms. Elise Deauxnim's staff!

Japanese: そして！　さらに重要なのは‥‥ 水晶が発見された場所です！
English: And even more important is the place where this crystal sphere was found!

Japanese: た、たしか‥‥‥上面図に 記されていたはずです！
English: In-Indeed... I believe it's already marked on this overhead map.

Japanese: 水晶が落ちていたのは‥‥ ‥‥ああああああッ！
English: The crystal was found... Aaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんです。
English: ...Precisely, Your Honor.

Japanese: そこは、まさに‥‥ 《振り子》の到達点なのです！
English: In the very spot where the pendulum would arrive if given the right amount of speed!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥‥ これで、立証されたようです。
English: This explains your theory quite well, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 《事件当夜‥‥いかにして、 死体が移動したのか‥‥》
English: You have provided us with a way the body could have been moved that night...

Japanese: 今回も、キワどいところ でしたな。‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: An impressive deduction, Mr. Wright. ...Most impressive.

Japanese: な‥‥‥成歩堂くんッ！
English: M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥その、ぬるいコーヒーで ネツをさますことだ‥‥
English: I thought this cold coffee might help cool you down.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですかッ！ ゴドー検事ィ！
English: Wh... What is the meaning of this, Mr. Godot!?

Japanese: ‥‥なかなか、苦味とコクのある スイリだったぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: That was a dark and bitter guess that you made, Trite.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。まだ１つだけ‥‥ 足りねえものがある。
English: ...But you forgot about one thing.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ なんですかっ！
English: Oh? And what would that be?
The aroma.
...Huh?

Japanese: 立ちのぼる深いアロマは コーヒーの最大の共犯者、だぜ‥‥
English: A coffee's most reliable accomplice is its deep and profound aroma...

Japanese: ‥‥あの、すみません。 わかるようにおねがいします。
English: Um... The rest of the court doesn't speak Coffeenese. Can you elaborate a bit more?

Japanese: 《犯人が、死体を対岸に飛ばした》 ‥‥それならば、当然！
English: If the criminal had sent the body to the other side like you say...

Japanese: その死体を“受け取った”人物‥‥ 《共犯者》がいる、ってコトだ。
English: ...then naturally, there must have been an accomplice laying in wait to catch it.

Japanese: 共犯者‥‥ッ！
English: An accomplice...!?

Japanese: ‥‥犯人自身は、おぼろ橋を わたることはできなかった。
English: ...The criminal wasn't able to cross Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: それならば‥‥いったい、 ダレに死体をたくしたのか‥‥？
English: So, who collected the body...?

Japanese: 聞かせてもらおうじゃねえか！ ‥‥まるほどうッ！
English: What do you have to say about that, Trite!?

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに、この犯行は ヒトリでは不可能です！
English: Mr. Godot is correct... This can't be the work of a single person!

Japanese: 弁護人！　よろしいですね？
English: Well, Mr. Wright!? You know what you must do.
...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （協力者がいなければ、死体が 葉桜院に移動するハズがない！）
English: (The body couldn't have made it to Hazakura Temple without an accomplice!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、うかがいます。
English: ...Very well then! If you please, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 《奥の院》の対岸で、死体を 受け取った人物とは、だれですか！
English: Who was the person that received the body on the Hazakura Temple side?

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ どうでしょうか、ゴドー検事。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Godot? I'm sure you have something to say.

Japanese: アンタの、今のアロマ‥‥
English: That aroma just now...

Japanese: ちょっと、ニオイが キツすぎたようだぜ。
English: It smelled of sulfur... Perhaps it was your chances of winning rotting away?

Japanese: （‥‥情報に まどわされてはダメだ！）
English: (I can't get my facts all mixed up like this!)

Japanese: （“共犯者”は、死体を受け取った あと、それを葉桜院に運び‥‥）
English: (After the accomplice received the body, they carried it to Hazakura Temple...)

Japanese: （七支刀で、細工をした！ それが、できた人物は‥‥）
English: (Then they altered it using the Shichishito! There's only one person it can be...!)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あんまり私を 見つめないように。
English: ...Mr. Wright. Please don't stare at me like that. It's creepy.

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 あなたですよ、もちろん。
English: It can only be you... Sister Iris.
Huh...?

Japanese: えええええええええッ！ あ‥‥あの‥‥私‥‥
English: Aaaaaaah! But I... I...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなに驚くことは ないと思いますけどね。
English: ...I don't see why you're so surprised.

Japanese: おぼろ橋から死体を運ぶには、 スノーモービルを使うしかない。
English: The only way to transport the body from Dusky Bridge is by snowmobile.

Japanese: ビキニさんは、致命的にコシを 痛めていて、死体など運べない。
English: But with her bad back, Sister Bikini could never pick up a body like that.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたしか、いないんです。
English: ...You're the only one that could have managed it.

Japanese: アンタ‥‥証人の話、 聞いていたかい‥‥？
English: Trite. Were you even listening to the witness's testimony...?

Japanese: このカワイコちゃんは、 事件当夜‥‥
English: On the night of the crime, this little cutie pie...

Japanese: 奥の院の中庭で、親子ゲンカの 後始末をしていたんだぜ。
English: ...was on clean-up duty in the Inner Temple Garden after the mother-daughter bloodbath.

Japanese: あなたこそ、お忘れですか？ ‥‥ゴドー検事。
English: I haven't forgotten. But have you, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: この証人は‥‥葉桜院でも 目撃されているんですよ。
English: This witness was also seen at Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 被害者の死体に 細工しているところを‥‥
English: ...desecrating the corpse of the victim.

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥ジツに奇妙です。 まるで、事件当夜‥‥
English: Strange indeed... It's almost as if...

Japanese: 葉桜院と奥の院‥‥ふたりの 被告人が存在したかのようです！
English: ...on that night, the defendant was in two different places at the same time!

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 １つだけ、聞かせてください。
English: ...Sister Iris. Let me ask you something...

Japanese: なぜ‥‥最初から 証言してくれなかったのですか？
English: Why didn't you mention it when you first gave your testimony?

Japanese: あ、あの。何をでしょうか‥‥？
English: M-Mention what?

Japanese: ‥‥モチロン、 《振り子》のことですよ。
English: The pendulum, of course.

Japanese: 事件当夜の現場の状況、 目撃証人のスケッチ‥‥
English: Using this sketch drawn by an eyewitness...

Japanese: 炎上した橋を使って死体をわたした 方法は、立証されました。
English: I have established how the body was moved using the burnt-out bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、それを 知っていたハズですからね。
English: Which means it's now a fact that this occurred, something you should've already known.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな！ 私、知りません！
English: N-No...! I... I had no idea!

Japanese: 《振り子》なんて‥‥ 思いつきもしませんでしたわ！
English: I-I didn't know anything about a pendulum!

Japanese: 死体の受けわたしには、あなたの 協力がゼッタイに必要でした。
English: But the body couldn't have been passed along to the other side without your help!

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、当然！
English: So you should have known about it!

Japanese: あなたは《振り子》のことを 知っていなければオカシイのです。
English: In fact, it'd be impossible for you to be clueless about this whole thing...

Japanese: ‥‥もし、あなたが‥‥ 本当に《あやめさん》ならば。
English: ...unless you're not really Iris to begin with.
What...!?

Japanese: な‥‥何を言いだすのですか！ 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: H-How can you say that, Mr. Wright...?

Japanese: な‥‥ナニをバカなことを！
English: What...!? What kind of nonsense is this?

Japanese: ‥‥こ、この証人が、 葉桜院　あやめではない‥‥
English: You... You're saying this witness isn't Iris of Hazakura Temple...?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まるほどう！ もしかして‥‥
English: A-Are you serious, Trite...!?

Japanese: こ‥‥‥コイツは‥‥‥ このオンナは‥‥ッ！
English: You... You mean... Th-This woman is...

Japanese: （あの晩‥‥死体を受け取ったのは あやめさん以外、あり得ない！）
English: (There's no one besides Iris that could have received the corpse that night!)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ハッキリしたな。 この“違和感”の正体が）
English: (Now I get it... Now I know why I've been sick to my stomach during this trial.)

Japanese: （‥‥彼女の証言が、きのうから 急に変わってしまった理由‥‥）
English: (Why her whole demeanor changed so suddenly from yesterday...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥真宵ちゃんに罪を 着せようとしている理由‥‥）
English: (...and why she's trying to pin this murder on Maya...)

Japanese: ‥‥たった今、 証言台に立っている人物‥‥
English: The woman that's standing there at the witness stand...

Japanese: この証人の、本当の名前はッ！
English: Her real name is...!

Japanese: （いや‥‥ちがうだろう！）
English: (No, wait! That can't be it!)

Japanese: （こんなところで まちがえるワケには行かない！）
English: (This is no time to be making stupid, amateurish mistakes!)

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたは今、 トンでもない告発をしています！
English: Mr. Wright! You've just made an absolutely absurd accusation!

Japanese: 悪ふざけはユルしませんぞ！
English: I won't have you turning my courtroom into a circus side-show!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！ （‥‥考えるまでもないだろう！）
English: Uuurgh...! (Come on! This one's a no-brainer!)

Japanese: ‥‥この名前を、二度と口に出す ことはないと思っていました。
English: I never thought I'd have to utter your name again...

Japanese: まして、本人を目の前にして。
English: Let alone see you.

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりですね。 美柳　ちなみさん。
English: ...It's been a long time, Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: ‥‥‥みやなぎ‥‥‥
English: Hawthorne...?

Japanese: あやめさんには、 双子の姉がいました。
English: Sister Iris had a twin sister.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、彼女。 美柳　ちなみです。
English: ...And you're looking at her. Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: いつか、どこかで‥‥ その名を聞いたような気がします。
English: That name rings some bells. Distant bells, but bells nonetheless...

Japanese: クッ‥‥ 気のせいじゃねえさ、ジイさん。
English: Ha...! It's just your imagination, gramps.

Japanese: ここに‥‥美柳　ちなみに 関するデータがあるぜ。
English: This file contains all the relevant data about Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: そうだったのですか‥‥ ５年前‥‥、あのときの！
English: Oh yes, I remember now... That case... 5 years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥お名前は、美柳　ちなみと 申しますわ。
English: My name is Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: ふつつか者ですが‥‥なにとぞ、 よろしくおねがいいたしますね。
English: I just want to say... It's an honor for me to be here in your noble presence.

Japanese: こちらこそ！
English: The honor is all mine!

Japanese: よろしくおねがいしますぞ！
English: No... The honor is all mine.

Japanese: ‥‥し、しかし！ この記録によると‥‥
English: B-But... According to this...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは‥‥‥ すでに死んでいるではないですか！
English: Dahlia Hawthorne... ...is already dead!

Japanese: ‥‥先月、死刑が 執行されたばかりです！
English: ...It says her execution was carried out last month!

Japanese: ‥‥“死”だって？ そんなもの‥‥‥
English: So what?

Japanese: この法廷じゃァ、 なんのイミもねえのさ！
English: Death has no meaning in this courtroom!

Japanese: なんですッとぉぉぉぉぉぉッ！
English: Wh-Whaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥ま、待ってください！
English: W-Wait a moment!

Japanese: 私のお姉さまは‥‥もう、 お亡くなりになったのですよ！
English: How can you...? My sister...! She's already dead!

Japanese: い、今さら‥‥
English: Wh-What kind of...?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたには、説明するまでも ないでしょう。
English: ...You of all people should already understand. After all...

Japanese: その身体には、倉院流霊媒道の 家元の血が流れているのですから。
English: The blood of the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique flows within that body.

Japanese: くらいんりゅう‥‥れいばい‥‥
English: The Kurain Channeling Technique...? Now where have I heard that...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。正確には、あなたは 《美柳　ちなみ》ではありません。
English: ...That's right. You're not Dahlia Hawthorne herself.

Japanese: 霊媒師の身体を借りた、 《霊体》なのですから！
English: You're the spirit of Dahlia, currently inhabiting the body of a spirit medium!

Japanese: ハナシとしてはオモシロイが‥‥ さすがに、ムリがあるぜ。
English: What an exciting story. Exciting, but quite impossible.

Japanese: 事件の晩、美柳　ちなみの霊が 霊媒された、ってワケかい？
English: You're asking us to buy that Dahlia Hawthorne just happened to be channeled by someone...

Japanese: 彼女の双子の妹・あやめがいる 《葉桜院》で‥‥
English: ...on the very night of the murder, to a temple where her twin sister, Iris was...?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: Well, if you're going to put things that way, then... Yes.

Japanese: そんなグーゼン、 あるわけがねえぜ！
English: We're supposed to believe a coincidence like that just happens!?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 グーゼンではありません。
English: Naturally it was no coincidence.

Japanese: すべては、《計画》どおり、 実行されていたのです。
English: The whole thing was part of a plan from the very beginning.

Japanese: この《指示書》に書かれている とおりに‥‥！
English: It's all written right here in these instructions...!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！　そ、それは‥‥
English: Ah...! Wh-What's that...!?

Japanese: あなたたちの母親、綾里　キミ子が 書いたものです。
English: These instructions were written by your mother, Morgan Fey!

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》が霊媒される ことは、最初から決まっていた！
English: And part of the plan called for Dahlia Hawthorne to be channeled!

Japanese: あの晩‥‥
English: That night...

Japanese: 葉桜院には、ふたりの 《あやめさん》が存在したのです。
English: ...there were two Irises at Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥“ふたり”‥‥だと？
English: ...T-Two of them?

Japanese: この手紙では、霊媒をする 時間が指定されています。
English: Even the time of the channeling was planned out.

Japanese: 『《消灯の鐘》が聞こえたら』‥‥ すなわち、午後１０時です。
English: "As soon as you hear the lights out bell"... In other words, 10:00 PM.

Japanese: しかし、夕食より以前から 《あやめさん》は、いました。
English: However, Iris was seen before dinnertime.

Japanese: つまり‥‥それは“ホンモノ” ということですね‥‥
English: That means the Iris that was at dinner was the real Iris.

Japanese: 彼女は、葉桜院で ぼくに、このずきんをくれました。
English: And the Iris who gave me this hood in the Main Hall was also the real Iris.

Japanese: つまり、ビキニさんが《奥の院》で 会ったという《あやめさん》は‥‥
English: Meaning that the Iris Sister Bikini saw at the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 修験者の装束を着た、別人‥‥ 《美柳　ちなみ》だったのです！
English: ...was someone else dressed as her! Namely, one Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: アンタ‥‥自分で言ってることが わかってるのかい？
English: Do you even know what you're saying, Trite?

Japanese: 事件当夜、《美柳　ちなみ》が 霊媒された‥‥
English: This whole "channeling the spirit of Dahlia Hawthorne" business...

Japanese: 計画書が残っている以上、 それは、たしかだろうぜ。
English: Yes, it's true that you found plans that talk about it.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！ これだけはハッキリしている。
English: ...However! There's one thing that's perfectly clear.

Japanese: そこの証言台に立っている証人は、 ホンモノの《あやめ》だぜ！
English: The witness currently standing in the witness stand is the real Iris!

Japanese: ‥‥‥な、なんですって‥‥‥！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 事件当夜のこと‥‥、落ちついて よく思い出してみな。
English: Calm down and remember what you know about the night of the crime.

Japanese: 霊媒された《美柳　ちなみ》は、 住職に会ったあと‥‥
English: After meeting Sister Bikini, the Dahlia Hawthorne that had been channeled...

Japanese: 落雷によって、《奥の院》に 閉じこめられてしまった。
English: ...would've been stranded at the Inner Temple due to the lightning strike.

Japanese: 死体が《振り子》で運ばれたのは、 それよりも後、だ。
English: It was later that the body was moved by pendulum.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなりますね。
English: ...That's right.

Japanese: このとき、対岸で死体を 受け取った《あやめ》は‥‥
English: Naturally that would mean that the Iris that received the body was...

Japanese: 当然、《本物》ということになる。 ‥‥ここまでは、いいか？
English: ...the real Iris. Are you with me so far?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: 事件の通報を受けて、警察は 葉桜院の本堂にかけつけた。
English: After being notified of what happened, the police came to Hazakura Temple's Main Hall.

Japanese: そして、自室にいた 《葉桜院　あやめ》を逮捕した。
English: There they found Iris in her room and arrested her.

Japanese: ‥‥それ以来、彼女は留置所で 警察の管理下にあった‥‥。
English: And ever since, she's been under police supervision at the detention center.

Japanese: た、たしかに‥‥ （そういうことになる‥‥な）
English: Y-Yes, I suppose... (I can't deny any of that...)

Japanese: ふう‥‥よかったですわ。
English: Whew... Thank goodness.

Japanese: やっと、ナットクして もらえたようですね‥‥
English: It looks like he's finally convinced...

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ やはり、何かがちがう！）
English: (But something still seems off... Way off...!)

Japanese: （今日の《あやめさん》は‥‥ あの晩の彼女とは、別人だ！）
English: I'm still not convinced that the Iris here is the same one from the other night.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
それなら、アンタ‥‥ こう主張するつもりかい？
English: I suppose you're about to say something really ridiculous?

Japanese: 本物の《あやめ》と、 霊媒された《ちなみ》‥‥
English: Like, the real Iris and the spirit of Dahlia...

Japanese: どこかで入れ替わった、 とでも‥‥？
English: ...somehow switched places?

Japanese: い、入れ替わった‥‥！
English: S-Switched places...!?

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ‥‥
English: To be perfectly honest...

Japanese: 私にはまだ、よく わからない点もありますが‥‥
English: ...there are still quite a few things I don't understand, but I do know...

Japanese: 証人の身元が確定しないかぎり、 審理を進めることは不可能です。
English: ...that unless we confirm the witness's identity, we can't continue with this trial.

Japanese: （‥‥あやめさんに、《ちなみ》を 霊媒する霊力は、ない）
English: (Iris doesn't have the spiritual power needed to channel Dahlia.)

Japanese: （そうなれば、たしかに‥‥）
English: (Which means...)

Japanese: （どこかで、入れ替わるしか ないわけか‥‥）
English: (...they must have switched places somewhere.)

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人！
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 葉桜院　あやめが逮捕されてから、 今日、証言台に立つまでの間‥‥
English: Since the time she was arrested at Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》と入れ替わる 機会は、あったのですか？
English: ...have there been any chances for Iris to switch places with Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: （お、思いつかない‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I can't think of one...)

Japanese: （逮捕された人間が、監視の目を 逃れられるハズがない！）
English: (She certainly couldn't have escaped while she was under arrest!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ アンタの、その目‥‥
English: Ha...! I love that look on your face!

Japanese: 山小屋にこもって、 イモでも食って暮らすかい？
English: Maybe you should go live in that old mountain shack and become a hermit?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥やはり、 そんな機会はなかったようですね。
English: It appears there was no opportunity for them to have switched places.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　‥‥ここで あきらめたら、負け犬だ‥‥）
English: (No! If I give up here, I'd never be able to live with myself...)

Japanese: （ムリヤリでもいい！ ‥‥可能性を探すんだ！）
English: (Even if it's a little forced, I have to think of something!)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: ...Your Honor!

Japanese: 一度だけ‥‥チャンスが あったかもしれません。
English: I think there might have been one chance.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと‥‥
English: Oh? Explain yourself...

Japanese: きのう‥‥数分間ですが、 空白の時間が存在したのです。
English: Yesterday for a few minutes, Iris's whereabouts were unknown.

Japanese: 空白の‥‥時間‥‥ どういうイミだ？
English: Unknown...? What do you mean?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥
English: What I mean is...

Japanese: あやめさんが、だれにも見られず 自由に行動できた時間ですよ。
English: There was a span of time in which Iris was able to move about freely, unsupervised.

Japanese: いったい、ダレですか！
English: Well, who was it!?

Japanese: 殺人事件の容疑者に、そんな 《自由》を与えたのは！
English: Who would give a murder suspect time to move about freely like that!?

Japanese: （‥‥悪いな。 おまえのせいじゃないけど‥‥）
English: (I'm sorry... I know you didn't mean to... It wasn't your fault...)

Japanese: あやめさんの《空白の時間》‥‥ それを与えた人物とは！
English: The person who gave Iris the chance to freely move about was...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: ...Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この法廷に、《空白の時間》を 作るワケには行きません！
English: There will be no unaccountability in my court, time or otherwise!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　アンタ‥‥
English: Ha...! Trite, Trite, Trite...

Japanese: きのうのことすら、キチンと おぼえてないのかい‥‥？
English: Can't you even remember what happened only yesterday...?

Japanese: （‥‥きのう、あやめさんは 《葉桜院》に護送されてきた）
English: (Yesterday, Iris was escorted to Hazakura Temple...)

Japanese: （‥‥修験洞の《錠》を 解除するために！）
English: (...in order to remove the locks on the Sacred Cavern!)

Japanese: （チャンスは‥‥ あのときしかなかった‥‥！）
English: (That would have been her only chance...!)

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥たしか。 御剣検事、ですな‥‥？
English: Th-This is... Mr. Edgeworth, isn't it...?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: ...Your Honor.

Japanese: きのう、大きな地震が ありましたね？
English: There was a fairly large earthquake yesterday, was there not?

Japanese: じしん‥‥ですか‥‥
English: An... earthquake? Hmm...

Japanese: じ、地震よおお‥‥ッ！
English: E-Earthquake!

Japanese: た‥‥タイヘン！ この揺れ‥‥奥の院が危ないよ！
English: Oh my goodness! The Inner Temple! This kind of tremor might...

Japanese: わ‥‥私としたことが‥‥
English: H-How could I have...?

Japanese: “脱走”、だ。 ‥‥取り逃がしてしまった。
English: She fled... She escaped!

Japanese: ぼくたちは、すぐに 《奥の院》へ向かいました。
English: We went to the Inner Temple right away.

Japanese: ‥‥そこには、たしかに 《あやめさん》がいました。
English: And it's true, Iris was already there.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: ‥‥すでに、 入れ替わりは終わっていた。
English: ...they had already switched places by that point in time.

Japanese: あのとき、修験堂にいたのは 《美柳　ちなみ》だったのです！
English: When I arrived at the Training Hall, I was met by none other than Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: い‥‥いいかげんに しなさいッ！　‥‥弁護人！
English: Th-That's quite enough already, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: よろしいですかッ！
English: Now see here!

Japanese: 《霊媒》などというものを 認めてしまっては、裁判など‥‥
English: No judge in his right mind would consider the idea of "spirit channeling" and...

Japanese: ‥‥おだまりなさいな。
English: ...Be quiet.

Japanese: おひさしぶりね‥‥ オジサマ。
English: It's been a long time... Mr. Judge.
Th-That voice...!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥あらためて、 聞く必要がありそうだな。
English: Guess I'll have to ask again.

Japanese: ‥‥カワイコちゃんには、 名前と職業を聞く。
English: ...Upon meeting a beautiful lady, always ask for her name and profession.

Japanese: それが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: That's one of my rules.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみよ。 今は‥‥‥死者、やってるわ。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne. And my current profession? Permanently retired.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
もう、正体をかくすつもりは ねえようだな。
English: So you're not going to bother hiding your identity anymore, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、そうね。 アタシ、もう死んでるから。
English: ...Why should I? After all, I'm dead.

Japanese: アンタたちには‥‥、もうアタシを 罰することはできないでしょ？
English: There's really nothing you can do to punish me!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんという‥‥
English: Wh-What is going on here...?

Japanese: （‥‥美柳　ちなみ‥‥）
English: (Dahlia Hawthorne...)

Japanese: （まさか‥‥こんな状況で 《再会》するなんて‥‥）
English: (I never thought we'd meet again. And I never thought we'd meet like this...)

Japanese: （今こそ‥‥決着をつけるんだ！ 彼女と、ぼく自身に‥‥）
English: (But this time, I'll end it! For her, and for myself...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
